nodes.label: anthropic-stem-fellowship
nodes.verdict:
**Substantial close-read pass on Q1 + Q2 + Q3 ¶1 landed (May 14); drafts now plain-text and paste-ready for Greenhouse.** Q1 rewritten across all three paragraphs — umbrella opener (weak gravitational lensing), chronological PhD→postdoc arc with "difficult reality of cosmological data" framing and 3–6-year analysis foreshadow, CEA Paris-Saclay credentials cluster with CNRS glossed at first mention. Q2 restructured into 2 paragraphs + handoff — ML knowledge + Bayesian/frequentist substrate + bias-vs-variance argument; word2vec/Foucault final project with ultimate-point sentence + TTS/STT + Pleias reframed for synthetic-data scaling. Q3 ¶1 added orienting sentence, replaced "results" with "null-test validation," added arXiv link inline, rewrote role-shift around "constructing back pressure that allows the agent to check its own work." CV: in-prep language replaced with "submitted to A&A"; publication counts synced at 4 further refereed. All markdown stripped from Q1–Q5 prose. **References (Lanusse + Holder) confirmed — Cail has been in touch.**

**Remaining for Cail:** close-read on Q3 ¶2–4 (Lightcone substrate, day-to-day workflow, multi-language tools), Q4 (three project ideas + Anthropic motivation + France–Anthropic closer), Q5 (reference context paragraphs). CV interactive walk-through still parked at [[application/anthropic-stem-fellowship/cv-rebuild]] (header shape, Lightcone wording trim, CNRS phrasing, any 2026 additions, Skills "AI & workflows" line). Optional INSPIRE eyeball. **Paste into Greenhouse and submit by Friday May 15.**

nodes.label: Anthropic STEM Fellowship — call requirements
nodes.verdict: Single source of truth for the application — 5 short-essay questions + CV + 2 references, no word limits, due May 15. Tight, focused, very different shape from CNRS.

nodes.label: CV rebuild — Anthropic STEM Fellowship
nodes.verdict:
Walkthrough pass with Cail landed. Standalone CV at `cv-rebuild/cv.tex` is in submission-ready shape (3 pages, compiles cleanly; `cv.pdf` is gitignored per the loom policy added this session — rebuild with `pdflatex cv.tex`).

**Settled this session:**
- **Title block**: "Cail Daley" in **EB Garamond** bold smallcaps (not Raleway), subheading *"Postdoctoral Researcher · Cosmology and Agentic Science"* below in smaller smallcaps, thin rule. Title-to-subtitle and subtitle-to-rule spacing balanced. Running header band dropped from all pages; centered page-number footers only.
- **Research Interests grid**: AI items lead (*Agentic science methodology* | *Verification of AI-mediated research*) in row 1; cosmology fills rows 2–3 (*Cosmic shear* | *CMB lensing & cross-correlations*; *Lensing systematics* | *Kinematic lensing*).
- **Citation line**: settled at **"h-index 19; ~1,800 citations to refereed publications"** — rounded up from the precise 1,769 in the refreshed snapshot [[bibliometrics-may-2026]]. INSPIRE-HEP and ORCID hyperlinked.
- **Lightcone bullet** (under *Agentic science — 2025–present*): *"Core team member of [Lightcone Research](https://lightconeresearch.org), a joint CNRS–Berkeley open-source project developing the [ASTRA](https://astra-spec.org) schema and surrounding infrastructure for reproducible, composable, and verifiable AI-mediated science. Maintain [felt](https://github.com/cailmdaley/felt), a Go CLI for tracking analysis decisions and findings as structured fibers."* Three teal hyperlinks; ASTRA correctly framed as Lightcone's deliverable, not a parallel project.
- **Workshop entry** at top of *Outreach & Service*: *"09/2026 — DAp SciAI Days, CEA Paris-Saclay (forthcoming): co-organize the department's three-day workshop on AI for scientific research; lead Day 2 on agentic research, including a live Claude Code demo and panel discussion."*
- **Loom gitignore policy** (separate commit `311712ff`): aggressive-ignore for `*.pdf`, `*.png`, `*.jpg`, `*.jpeg` + LaTeX scratch. `cv.pdf` removed from tracking. Existing PNGs across the loom were *not* mass-untracked — Cail flagged that decision as a follow-up audit.

**Open follow-ups (none blocking submission):**
1. **Q3 consistency in `ai-futures/anthropic-stem/drafts.md`**: currently says *"core contributor to Lightcone Research"*; should swap to *"core team member"* to match the CV's framing.
2. **Skills "AI & workflows" row**: surfaced as candidate for harder Claude Code emphasis; not changed this pass (researcher-not-builder anchor weighed against pitch-deck adjacency).
3. **Daley+26 publication entry**: surfaced as candidate (currently absorbed into the bibliometrics summary line); not broken out.
4. **Portolan public Friday**: if it ships before submission, a fourth hyperlink could land — explicitly scoped out of this pass to avoid a half-broken link risk.
5. **Existing tracked PNGs in the loom** (~30 across portolan/dossier/analysis-frontispiece): policy now says default-ignore, but existing tracked files weren't bulk-removed. Separate audit pass when Cail wants.

nodes.label: Application drafts
nodes.verdict: Five textareas + Q5 references; close-reading in progress.
